title: starkea umbellata
intro: Starkea umbellata, also known as the starflower or lesser star of Bethlehem, is a flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Starkea umbellata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and soothing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
